title: pedicularis cenisia proles hugueninii
intro: Pedicularis cenisia proles hugueninii is a rare alpine plant species that belongs to the Pedicularis genus, commonly known as lousewort or woodruff, which is a type of flowering plant found in high mountainous regions. The medicinal properties of Pedicularis cenisia proles hugueninii include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and act as an expectorant, making it a potential remedy for coughs, colds, and bronchitis. In traditional herbal medicine, Pedicularis cenisia proles hugueninii is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to utilize its medicinal properties.
